title: Treacle and ginger tart
subtitle: A deep, dark treacle filling sharpened with ginger in a shortcrust case
excerpt: The classic treacle tart enriched with dark treacle alongside golden syrup, ground ginger for warmth, and lemon for sharpness. A richer, darker take on the British nursery classic.
sourceNotes: Treacle tart is a British classic documented in Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136. The original recipe uses golden syrup and breadcrumbs as the filling; this version adds black treacle for depth and ground ginger for warmth, following a tradition of spiced treacle tarts documented across northern English baking literature of the nineteenth and twentieth centuries.

content.text: The standard British treacle tart is made with golden syrup, breadcrumbs, and lemon. This version adds black treacle to the filling, which deepens the flavour from sweet to complex, and increases the ground ginger from a pinch to a full teaspoon. The result is a darker, more savoury tart, still unmistakeably treacle tart but less one-note.

case is blind baked before the filling goes in; this keeps the base crisp under the wet filling. The filling sets to a soft, fudgy consistency when baked and remains slightly sticky when cold. Serve warm with clotted cream or cold with a sharp crème fraîche.

attrs.prepNote: cold, cut into pieces, for the pastry

content.text: Make the pastry and blind bake

Rub {{unsalted-butter}} into {{plain-flour}} and {{icing-sugar}} until the mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add {{egg-yolks}} and 1 to 2 tablespoons of cold water; mix to a firm dough. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Roll out and line a 23 cm loose-bottomed tart tin. Refrigerate for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Line the case with baking paper and fill with baking beans. Blind bake for 15 minutes, then remove paper and beans and bake for a further 5 minutes until the base is dry and pale.

Warm {{golden-syrup}} and {{black-treacle}} gently in a saucepan until fluid; do not boil. Remove from the heat and stir in {{double-cream}}, {{ginger-ground}}, the zest and juice from {{lemon}}, and {{eggs}}. Stir briefly to combine. Pour into the blind-baked case. Bake at 170°C fan for 20 to 25 minutes until the filling is set at the edges with a very slight wobble in the centre; it should read about 80°C on a probe thermometer at the edge. Cool for at least 30 minutes before slicing.
The tart sets further as it cools. Slightly wobbly from the oven is correct; it will firm to a sliceable consistency within 30 minutes.
For the traditional version without ginger: use 350 g of golden syrup, no black treacle, and increase the lemon zest to two lemons. The filling will be sweeter and more golden.
The traditional filling uses fresh breadcrumbs rather than eggs and cream. Breadcrumb version: 350 g golden syrup + 75 g black treacle + 100 g fresh breadcrumbs + lemon zest and juice + ginger.
